DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT AJAX BOILER, INC.S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET 1; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET 1; REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET 1; REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET 1 AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE COURT FINDS THAT AS A RESULT OF AJAX BOILER,INC'S SERVICE OF SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES UPON PLAINTIFF ON OCTOBER 21, 2014, IT WAS NOT POSSIBLE FOR PLAINTIFF TO REVIEW THE SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES BEFORE THE DEADLINE FOR THE FILING OF PLAINTIFF'S REPLY BRIEF, THUS DEPRIVING PLAINTIFF OF THE OPPORTUNITY TO POSSIBLY NARROW THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES IN DISPUTE. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ROBERT KANE, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AJAX BOILER, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Anderson, Rowe & Buckley, Inc.'s Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions - Defendant Ajax Boiler, Inc.'s ("Ajax") Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and Special Interrogatories is granted. Ashley Washington of Call & Jensen APC (949-717-3000) CourtCall appearance for defendant Ajax Boiler opposing party.

Discussion, after met and conferred. The Court rules PLAINTIFF ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Ajax Boiler, Inc.S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1; Special Interrogatories, Set 1; Requests For Production Of Documents, Set 1; Request For Admissions, Set 1 And For Monetary Sanctions. The Court finds that as a result of Ajax Boiler,Inc's service of supplemental discovery responses upon Plaintiff on October 21, 2014, it was not possible for plaintiff to review the supplemental discovery responses before the deadline for the filing of plaintiff's reply brief, thus depriving plaintiff of the opportunity to possibly narrow the discovery responses in dispute. Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHLOE MUSSELMAN OF CLAPP MORONEY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 989-5400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ASHLEY M. WASHINGTON OF CALL & JENSEN, APC, TEL. NO. (949) 717-3000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AJAX BOILER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - The court discloses that counsel for R.F. MacDonald previously worked for the court. The court believes that this prior employment is not a ground for disqualification. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. The trial has been continued and the parties will have an opportunity to conduct discovery and defendants will have an opportunity to file motions for summary judgment and will not be prejudiced. This motion to amend is not the proper vehicle to resolve the economic loss and equitable tolling issues. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
